## How Ava runs outbound   autonomously

Set your outbound strategy once. Ava finds the leads, runs the campaigns, and books the meetings.

1

### She finds and prioritizes high-intent leads

Search 250M+ verified B2B contacts or import from your CRM. Ava enriches prospects using 22+ data sources, then prioritizes them by likelihood to convert using intent signals like funding rounds and leadership hires.

2

### She launches personalized multi-channel sequences

Ava sends personalized multi-channel outreach across email and social. She queues call steps in the native dialer so your reps can click and dial without leaving the platform.

3

### She tests and optimizes messaging automatically

Ava runs dozens of message variations per campaign simultaneously, testing subject lines, structure, CTAs, and tone. She shifts volume toward what's working so performance compounds over time.

4

### She handles replies and books meetings autonomously

Ava handles every reply. She qualifies interested leads, handles objections, and books meetings directly on your reps' calendars. You set escalation rules to decide when a human gets pulled in.

![She finds and prioritizes high-intent leads](https://www.artisan.co/_next/image?url=%2Fassets%2Fhome%2Fnew%2Fava-autonomous-1.webp&w=3840&q=75&dpl=dpl_7DtTFreMMxsrZ3YMT5L4rL1YyAGD)

# We're redefining the future of work with AI employees

The era of manual, repetitive tasks is over. We're building Artisans—intuitive, human-like AI collaborators designed to handle the tedious, low-leverage work. So your team can focus on what truly matters: solving big problems, driving growth, and doing work they love.

![About Artisan](https://www.artisan.co/_next/image?url=%2Fassets%2Fabout%2Fabout-hero.webp&w=3840&q=75&dpl=dpl_9A5YsuHta5rGPHyoLQtCBsTr2G1F)

## We believe work should be fulfilling, not frustrating.

Today's software landscape is broken: fragmented tools, manual workflows, outdated technology. It drains creativity and makes work dull.

![We believe work should be fulfilling, not frustrating.](https://www.artisan.co/_next/image?url=%2Fassets%2Fabout%2Fabout-artisan-1.webp&w=3840&q=75&dpl=dpl_9A5YsuHta5rGPHyoLQtCBsTr2G1F)

## Our recipe for success

At Artisan, these are the core values that inspire the way we work.

![Reliability](https://www.artisan.co/assets/about/recipe1.svg?dpl=dpl_9A5YsuHta5rGPHyoLQtCBsTr2G1F)

### Reliability

Be reliable. If you say you're going to do something, do it.

![Excellence](https://www.artisan.co/assets/about/recipe2.svg?dpl=dpl_9A5YsuHta5rGPHyoLQtCBsTr2G1F)

### Excellence

Raise your standards. Aim for insanely great. Do not lower your standards to move things along and get them off your desk. Take the Steve Jobs approach.

![Customer](https://www.artisan.co/assets/about/recipe3.svg?dpl=dpl_9A5YsuHta5rGPHyoLQtCBsTr2G1F)

### Customer

The customer is at the center of everything we do, and they must have an exceptional experience.

![Impatience](https://www.artisan.co/assets/about/recipe4.svg?dpl=dpl_9A5YsuHta5rGPHyoLQtCBsTr2G1F)

### Impatience

Constantly apply pressure, be impatient, and strive for speed. This is a race.

![Pioneering](https://www.artisan.co/assets/about/recipe5.svg?dpl=dpl_9A5YsuHta5rGPHyoLQtCBsTr2G1F)

### Pioneering

Do not settle for the status quo. We are pioneering the next paradigm of software, and that should reflect in everything that we do.

![Execution](https://www.artisan.co/assets/about/recipe6.svg?dpl=dpl_9A5YsuHta5rGPHyoLQtCBsTr2G1F)

### Execution

Strategy and ideas are useless without formidable execution. Execute relentlessly.

## FAQ

### How does the free trial work?

Each new account starts with a 10K credit free trial (~$300 in value) and 30 days to use them. You get access to all features with no credit card required. The 10K credit grant is available to new customers only and limited to one per company (additional teammates share the existing trial). If you don't enter a credit card by the end of the trial, your account automatically downgrades to the Free plan, and all campaigns will be paused. Credits cannot be transferred or redeemed for cash.

### What are credits and how do they work?

Credits power Ava's work, from finding and enriching leads to personalizing outreach and handling replies. Simpler actions like email enrichment cost less; more intensive ones like deploying swarms of research agents cost more. Your plan includes a credit allocation that covers each billing period. Sending infrastructure like mailboxes and phone numbers is billed separately in dollars.

### How many credits does a typical campaign use?

It depends on your campaign type, the channels you use, how much personalization you want, and the length of your sequence, so it's quite flexible. Most campaigns start at around 20 credits per prospect enrolled, covering enrichment and outreach. Bringing your own lead list or using templated messages costs less; using advanced signals and longer, ultra-personalized sequences costs more.

### How do I pick the right plan?

Start with the campaign type you'll run and how many leads you want to reach each month. Use the Credits Estimator to see roughly how many credits that requires, then pick the plan that covers it. You can always upgrade as you scale without losing any data.

### If I'm on an annual plan, do I get all my credits upfront?

Yes! Annual plans give you your entire credit allocation at the start of your term, so you have full flexibility to use them whenever you need throughout the year.

### What happens if I run out of credits?

You'll get a warning as your balance gets low. Credits for each lead are spent when the lead is enrolled, so sequences already in progress will continue uninterrupted. New enrollments pause when credits are exhausted so there are no surprise charges. You can purchase additional credits instantly to resume, or upgrade to a higher plan at any time.

### Do unused credits roll over?

On monthly plans, unused credits carry over until the end of the following billing period. On annual plans, you receive your full credit allocation upfront, and any unused credits at the end of your term do not roll over.

### Can I upgrade or downgrade at any time?

Yes. Upgrades take effect immediately and you get instant access to the additional credits. Downgrades take effect at the end of your current billing cycle, and you can cancel a pending downgrade any time before it kicks in.

### What's included in the Free plan?

The Free plan gives you 300 credits/month to explore the platform. You can search leads, run enrichments, and use agent swarm research. Campaigns, deliverability tools, and integrations require a paid plan (Intern or above).

## How we got here

### Restoring trust in internet businesses

Vanta was founded in 2018, in the wake of several high-profile data breaches. Online security was only becoming more important, but we knew firsthand how hard it could be for fast-growing companies to invest the time and manpower it takes to build a solid security foundation. Vanta was inspired by a vision to restore trust in internet businesses by enabling companies to improve and prove their security.

### We started by automating

From our early days automating security monitoring for compliance standards like SOC 2, HIPAA and ISO 27001 to creating the world's leading Agentic Trust Platform, our vision remains unchanged. Now more than ever, making security continuous—not just a point-in-time check— is essential. Thousands of companies rely on Vanta to build, maintain and demonstrate their trust— all in a way that's real-time and transparent.

### Christina Cacioppo

### CEO & Founder

Christina Cacioppo is the CEO and co-founder of Vanta,  the leading Agentic Trust Platform backed by Sequoia Capital, Craft Ventures, Y Combinator, J.P. Morgan and Goldman Sachs. Christina founded Vanta in 2018 after experiencing firsthand the manual and time consuming process of getting SOC 2 during her time leading Dropbox Paper.

Today, over 16,000 companies rely on Vanta to build, maintain and demonstrate their trust—all in a way that's real-time and transparent. Prior to founding Vanta, Christina led product management for Dropbox Paper and was a member of the investment team for Union Square Ventures. Christina received her B.A. in Economics and M.S. in Management Science and Engineering from Stanford University.

### Stevie Case

### Chief Revenue Officer

Stevie oversees Vanta’s go-to-market team to support the company’s rapid growth. Stevie brings over 15 years of sales and business development experience to the new role, most recently serving as Vice President of Mid-Market Sales at Twilio, a market-leading cloud communications platform.

Joining as one of their first account executives, Stevie played a pivotal role in establishing Twilio’s enterprise business with key Fortune 500 customers, generating more than $400 million in annual recurring revenue. She helped to grow the sales team from a dozen to over 1,000 team members. Stevie also supports startups, serving as an angel investor and advisor for the past decade.

### Scott Holden

### Chief Marketing Officer

Scott Holden is Vanta's Chief Marketing Officer, leading Vanta’s efforts to expand global awareness of its market-leading trust management platform.

With 20 years of marketing leadership at high-growth SaaS companies including Brex, ThoughtSpot, and Salesforce, Scott has driven category creation in competitive, fast-evolving markets. During his tenure as CMO at Brex, Scott evolved the company’s brand into the leading AI-powered spend platform including Brex’s high-impact brand campaigns featuring companies like DoorDash, Robinhood, and the Boston Celtics. Prior to Brex, Scott served as CMO and Head of GTM at ThoughtSpot and held multiple marketing leadership roles at Salesforce, including VP of Marketing for the Salesforce Platform, Sales Cloud and Chatter. Scott has a B.A. in Economics and Philosophy from Colgate University and an MBA in Marketing from Stanford University.

### Jeremy Epling

### Chief Product Officer

Jeremy Epling is Vanta’s Chief Product Officer overseeing engineering, product and design. Bringing over two decades of building to Vanta, Jeremy joins Vanta from GitHub where he was VP, Product for Actions, Codespaces, npm, Packages, leading all things Code to Cloud. During his tenure, Jeremy more than quadrupled the engineering, product and design teams, as GitHub Actions scaled from zero to millions of global developers. He also led GitHub Codespaces, GitHub Packages, and npm, delivering security features such as supply chain security, improving and requiring MFA, and new provenance capabilities for npm.

Prior to GitHub, Jeremy spent over 16 years at Microsoft, last serving as Principal Group Product Manager for Azure, empowering developers to continuously build, test, and deploy to any platform or cloud. He studied Computer Science at the University of North Carolina, Chapel Hill.

## Our values are our  operating principles

Vanta’s mission is to protect consumer data and restore trust in internet business. To do that, we’re building an enduring company where all Vanta’ns can do the best work of their careers. Building that enduring company together necessitates articulating how we work and how we make decisions - we call these our principles.

## Our Principles

Vanta’s mission is to help businesses earn and prove trust. To do that, we’re building an enduring company where all Vanta’ns can do the best work of their careers, which requires us to articulate, and regularly revisit with honesty and clarity, how we work and how we make decisions - we call these our “Principles.”

We use these Principles every day in how we operate and build Vanta, and we also screen all of our hires for them . The Principles are the core tenets that guide how we work, how we hire, and how we interact with customers and partners.

As Vanta grows, we’ll update our thinking and revise these Principles, balancing “what got us here” with what’s likely to propel us further.

![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/64009032676f24f376f002fc/641b79b60e91ad268c4f3a81_icon-twistyarrow.svg)

## A Vanta’n will...

![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/64009032676f24f376f002fc/641387ef428bdf01130b01de_Customers%20First_Dark%20Purple.svg)

Put our customers first

We center our customers and their needs in our work, and invest in long-term relationships with them. What and how we build is driven by our customers first and foremost, so that we’re more likely to achieve our mission of securing the internet.

![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/64009032676f24f376f002fc/641387f03365e3a21c20ec0a_Bias%20for%20action_Dark%20Purple.svg)

Bias for action

All Vanta’ns bias for action, which means that they make decisions and act quickly even in the face of uncertainty, and are willing to make mistakes in the process. We know that most decisions are “two-way doors.” When we get new information, we’re comfortable with reversing decisions as long as we move quickly so we can iterate and learn.

![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/64009032676f24f376f002fc/641387f5bf81748652061e82_Heart_Dark%20Purple.svg)

Win as one team

Vanta’ns prioritize the whole of Vanta in their work, and will put their team (both the “team” of Vanta the company, and the team(s) they work with) before themselves when doing their work. We celebrate the successes of our teammates, knowing that those successes are also our own, and we presume good intent because we know that our teammates are also putting Vanta, and our customers, first.

![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/64009032676f24f376f002fc/641387ef04e9be08b5d6ac48_Frameworks_Dark%20Purple.svg)

Decide with frameworks

Vanta’ns are known for biasing to action and making quick decisions - but they’re also known for making those decisions thoughtfully. Vanta’ns make decisions using frameworks, applicable to Vanta as a whole, by creating a set of decision criteria, considering alternatives and impact, and clearly articulating the process behind and support for each of their decisions. Vanta’ns also document decisions in artifacts that are accessible to their colleagues, and make sure that critical decisions follow a decision-making framework.

![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/64009032676f24f376f002fc/642473ce70faa44ea2f418f4_Lead-with-resilience_Dark%20purple.svg)

Lead with resilience

Vanta created and leads a dynamic, enormous market, and we operate in incredibly high stakes situations helping to keep our customers, and their customers, safe and secure. As such, Vanta’ns often find themselves in rapidly changing situations where expectations and direction are unclear, and where external parties may not practice our principles. We don’t let rough seas knock us off course - instead, we operate with resilience and grit. We see rapid change, and our ability to respond to it quickly and effectively, as an opportunity to continue redefining the industries we operate in to serve our customers.

![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/64009032676f24f376f002fc/641387f392dd4d9e32fa8e20_Tin_Dark%20Purple.svg)

Do what it says on the tin

We are building a trust management platform to allow all companies to manage and demonstrate their security continuously, and that mission requires trust. For Vanta to earn and keep that trust, each Vanta’n must hold themselves to the highest standards of ethics and accountability, and be transparent, truthful, and humble, especially when we inevitably make mistakes along the way.

## How we got here

### Restoring trust in internet businesses

Vanta was founded in 2018, in the wake of several high-profile data breaches. Online security was only becoming more important, but we knew firsthand how hard it could be for fast-growing companies to invest the time and manpower it takes to build a solid security foundation. Vanta was inspired by a vision to restore trust in internet businesses by enabling companies to improve and prove their security.

### We started by automating

From our early days automating security monitoring for compliance standards like SOC 2, HIPAA and ISO 27001 to creating the world's leading Agentic Trust Platform, our vision remains unchanged. Now more than ever, making security continuous—not just a point-in-time check— is essential. Thousands of companies rely on Vanta to build, maintain and demonstrate their trust— all in a way that's real-time and transparent.

### Christina Cacioppo

### CEO & Founder

Christina Cacioppo is the CEO and co-founder of Vanta,  the leading Agentic Trust Platform backed by Sequoia Capital, Craft Ventures, Y Combinator, J.P. Morgan and Goldman Sachs. Christina founded Vanta in 2018 after experiencing firsthand the manual and time consuming process of getting SOC 2 during her time leading Dropbox Paper.

Today, over 16,000 companies rely on Vanta to build, maintain and demonstrate their trust—all in a way that's real-time and transparent. Prior to founding Vanta, Christina led product management for Dropbox Paper and was a member of the investment team for Union Square Ventures. Christina received her B.A. in Economics and M.S. in Management Science and Engineering from Stanford University.

### Stevie Case

### Chief Revenue Officer

Stevie oversees Vanta’s go-to-market team to support the company’s rapid growth. Stevie brings over 15 years of sales and business development experience to the new role, most recently serving as Vice President of Mid-Market Sales at Twilio, a market-leading cloud communications platform.

Joining as one of their first account executives, Stevie played a pivotal role in establishing Twilio’s enterprise business with key Fortune 500 customers, generating more than $400 million in annual recurring revenue. She helped to grow the sales team from a dozen to over 1,000 team members. Stevie also supports startups, serving as an angel investor and advisor for the past decade.

### Scott Holden

### Chief Marketing Officer

Scott Holden is Vanta's Chief Marketing Officer, leading Vanta’s efforts to expand global awareness of its market-leading trust management platform.

With 20 years of marketing leadership at high-growth SaaS companies including Brex, ThoughtSpot, and Salesforce, Scott has driven category creation in competitive, fast-evolving markets. During his tenure as CMO at Brex, Scott evolved the company’s brand into the leading AI-powered spend platform including Brex’s high-impact brand campaigns featuring companies like DoorDash, Robinhood, and the Boston Celtics. Prior to Brex, Scott served as CMO and Head of GTM at ThoughtSpot and held multiple marketing leadership roles at Salesforce, including VP of Marketing for the Salesforce Platform, Sales Cloud and Chatter. Scott has a B.A. in Economics and Philosophy from Colgate University and an MBA in Marketing from Stanford University.

### Jeremy Epling

### Chief Product Officer

### Jeremy Epling

### Chief Product Officer

Jeremy Epling is Vanta’s Chief Product Officer overseeing engineering, product and design. Bringing over two decades of building to Vanta, Jeremy joins Vanta from GitHub where he was VP, Product for Actions, Codespaces, npm, Packages, leading all things Code to Cloud. During his tenure, Jeremy more than quadrupled the engineering, product and design teams, as GitHub Actions scaled from zero to millions of global developers. He also led GitHub Codespaces, GitHub Packages, and npm, delivering security features such as supply chain security, improving and requiring MFA, and new provenance capabilities for npm.

Prior to GitHub, Jeremy spent over 16 years at Microsoft, last serving as Principal Group Product Manager for Azure, empowering developers to continuously build, test, and deploy to any platform or cloud. He studied Computer Science at the University of North Carolina, Chapel Hill.

email_a.subject: Manual vendor research bottleneck
email_a.body: Building an AI wedding platform while manually researching and vetting vendors creates a major operational bottleneck that limits your ability to scale quality recommendations. We built Ava, an AI BDR that automates vendor outreach, qualification, and partnership development - essentially doing what you're likely doing manually to build your vendor network. Since you're already using AI to help couples plan weddings, this could free up your team to focus on product development instead of endless vendor acquisition calls. Worth a 15-min call this week?

email_b.subject: Scaling after your media coverage
email_b.body: Congrats on the recent features in NYT and other outlets - that kind of visibility typically drives a surge in wedding vendor inquiries wanting to be listed on your platform. We help AI companies like yours scale vendor partnerships through Ava, our AI BDR that handles outreach and qualification automatically. Given that you're already saving couples 500+ hours with AI, this could help you capitalize on your media momentum without overwhelming your team with partnership calls. Worth a 15-min call this week?
